title: Human Development Index groups
subtitle: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.
caption:
Human Development Index groups

The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.

This choropleth world map displays 2023 Human Development Index groups categorized into four distinct levels: Low, Medium, High, and Very high. The legend assigns red to the Low category, yellow to Medium, light blue to High, and dark blue to Very high. The Very high category, represented by the darkest blue, covers North America, most of Western and Northern Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, and parts of South America including Argentina and Chile. The High category, shown in light blue, includes Russia, China, Brazil, Mexico, and several nations in Eastern Europe and the Middle East. The Medium category, colored yellow, encompasses India, Indonesia, Egypt, and a significant portion of countries across Sub-Saharan Africa and Southeast Asia. The Low category, indicated by red, is concentrated in a central band across Africa, including nations such as Niger, Chad, and the Central African Republic, as well as Yemen and Afghanistan. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data is available. The visual distribution shows a concentration of the highest development groups in the Northern Hemisphere's western and eastern extremities and Oceania, while the lowest groups are clustered in specific regions of Africa and Asia.
